feed.title: HealthRankings — Health News
feed.subtitle: Latest US health headlines. Full list: https://healthrankings.co/healthrankings-news.html

entry.title: Hantavirus cluster linked to cruise ship travel, Multi-country
entry.summary: On 2 May 2026, a cluster of passengers with severe respiratory illness aboard a cruise ship was reported to the World Health Organization. The ship is carrying 147 passengers and crew. As of 4 May 2026, seven cases (two laboratory confirmed cases of hantaviru…

entry.title: What to know about hantavirus, the illness suspected in a cruise ship outbreak
entry.summary: Three people have died and others have been sickened after what some health authorities suspect may be a rare hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship in the Atlantic Ocean. Hantaviruses exist throughout the world and are spread mainly by contact with rodents. It…

entry.title: Cruise ship outbreaks hit 23 in 2025, with norovirus the most common virus
entry.summary: While taking a cruise is generally safe, having a high concentration of people in a semienclosed space makes it easy for viruses to spread among passengers.

entry.title: Cruise ship outbreaks hit 23 in 2025, with norovirus the most common virus
entry.summary: While taking a cruise is generally safe, having a high concentration of people in a semienclosed space makes it easy for viruses to spread among passengers.

entry.title: Inside Jordan Peterson’s ‘brutally painful’ illness as his wife reveals he’s suffering serious side effects from benzo withdrawal
entry.summary: “He misses the opportunity to weigh in on societal issues,” Tammy Peterson said of her husband.

entry.title: Patients remain cancer-free nearly 3 years after receiving experimental immunotherapy
entry.summary: Every participant in a new bowel cancer immunotherapy trial remained cancer-free nearly three years after receiving pembrolizumab before surgery.

entry.title: New tool can find deadliest cancer years before tumors can be seen on a scan
entry.summary: Many are split on whether AI is good for humanity, but a new AI-powered tool could prove revolutionary when it comes to cancer screenings.

entry.title: The dark side of the American work ethic: widespread sleep deprivation, linked to obesity, depression, even early death
entry.summary: From billions in lost productivity to rising healthcare costs, a widespread sleep deficit is undermining the American economy.

entry.title: Cranberry juice could slow the emergence of drug-resistant bacteria
entry.summary: The rise of bacteria is making it harder to combat conditions such as urinary tract infections

entry.title: Dads are dying after their kids are born, and no one is tracking it
entry.summary: New study examines paternal mortality data in Georgia, finds 60% of deaths were preventable

entry.title: This simple amino acid supplement greatly reduces Alzheimer’s damage
entry.summary: A new study suggests a surprisingly simple compound could help fight Alzheimer’s disease. Researchers found that arginine—an inexpensive amino acid already considered safe—can reduce the buildup of toxic amyloid proteins in the brain, a hallmark of the diseas…

entry.title: Omega-3 Supplements Linked to Cognitive Decline in Surprising New Study
entry.summary: Omega-3 supplements are widely regarded as a simple way to support brain health, especially with age.

entry.title: One in Five at Risk: Scientists Identify a Hidden Blood Particle Linked to Higher Risk of Stroke and Death
entry.summary: A hidden cholesterol particle may signal overlooked cardiovascular risk, offering a new clue for prevention and future treatments.

entry.title: Doctor shares visible body part that can give away size of penis
entry.summary: Move over hands and feet, as a new penis comparison metric has been revealed

entry.title: Ghanaian student receives top accounting award in US
entry.summary: Eric Ati, a Ghanaian alumnus of the University of Ghana Business School (UGBS), has been named the recipient of the Marcotte Family Outstanding Master of Science in Accountancy Award at Illinois State University in the United States.

entry.title: 5 Nutrients You Need More of as You Age, According to Dietitians
entry.summary: You need more calcium, protein, vitamin D, vitamin B12, and fiber as you age. Here, experts explain why—and how you can work more of each into your diet.

entry.title: Women’s bodies change across their lifespan. Should their nutrition?
entry.summary: Evidence-based nutritional strategies at each phase of the female lifespan can support transitions, prevent chronic disease and promote long-term well-being.

entry.title: Why Do We Fear Heights? An Evolutionary Biologist Explains The Hardwired Response
entry.summary: The fear that grips you at the edge of a balcony is older than language, older than memory. It’s millions of years of evolution compressed into a single glance downward.

entry.title: Biological age tests reveal what slows or hastens aging – but they’re useful only for researchers, not consumers
entry.summary: Epigenetic aging clocks allow scientists to study how populations age. They aren’t intended for individuals to make health decisions.

entry.title: Column | Can vaping cause cancer? The evidence suggests it might.
entry.summary: A research review links vaping to lung cancer. As an oncologist, here’s what I tell people when they ask me about vapes and cancer risk.

entry.title: OpenAI Made a Special ChatGPT for Your Doctor
entry.summary: ChatGPT for Clinicians is meant to help health care providers with daily tasks. And it's free for verified providers.

entry.title: Doctors weigh in on Ozempic, Wegovy, plus new ‘dual-action’ shot
entry.summary: TAMPA, Fla. (WFLA)— These drugs fall into a class known as GLP-1s, mimicking a natural hormone that helps regulate blood sugar, slows how quickly food leaves the stomach, and signals the brain that you’re full. “I would say in this practice, probably 70% of m…

entry.title: Vision problem leads to man's stage 4 lung cancer diagnosis, new drug extends survival
entry.summary: A former Ironman triathlete says his eye problems led to a shocking stage 4 lung cancer diagnosis, but a series of targeted drugs has helped him beat the odds.

entry.title: Exclusive | People are doing ‘rage workouts’ to blow through their anger — and fuel their fitness
entry.summary: Ever get so mad you could just… run a mile? Anger can be fiery fuel for a serious sweat session — and that exercise, in turn, can help blow off some steam.

entry.title: Ancient Document Confirms “Legendary” African King Actually Existed
entry.summary: A small, newly uncovered document from ancient Dongola is reshaping what historians know about a little-understood period in Sudan’s past.

entry.title: Doctors and Nurses at Schneider Hospital Sued After Patient’s Death Following Pneumonia Admission
entry.summary: Several doctors and nurses at the Schneider Regional Medical Center are being sued for malpractice f...

entry.title: Science Has Found Even More Ways Coffee Is Good for You
entry.summary: A new study shows the mechanisms of how coffee modifies the microbiome, reduces inflammation, and influences mood. Even decaf has its perks.

entry.title: Scientists reveal creatine’s hidden power beyond muscle gains
entry.summary: Creatine might be famous in the gym, but its real story is far more interesting. Naturally produced in the body, it helps power cells by rapidly regenerating ATP—the fuel that keeps muscles, the brain, and even the heart running during intense activity. Suppl…

entry.title: A physical therapist says you should work on strengthening this often overlooked area if you want to age well and stay independent
entry.summary: These three simple moves can improve your grip strength

entry.title: One Type of Exercise Can Give You Better Results With Less Effort, Expert Says
entry.summary: Exercising smarter rather than harder could lead to more effective muscle training, offering those of us short on time or energy an alternative way to maximize gains from workouts.

entry.title: I noticed last month that I have been turning down invitations not because I don't want to go, but because saying yes used to mean rearranging my life around someone else's plan, and I'm still flinching at a contract nobody is asking me to sign anymore
entry.summary: I kept declining invitations I actually wanted to accept. Then I noticed the reflex was protecting me from a deal nobody was offering — a contract I'd signed in childhood that had quietly expired without my body being told.

entry.title: Beyond Inflammation: Scientists Uncover New Cause of Persistent Rheumatoid Arthritis
entry.summary: Scar-driven changes in joint tissue may underlie treatment resistance in rheumatoid arthritis, opening the door to new targeted therapies.

entry.title: How Perimenopause Became America's New Health Scare
entry.summary: Influencers are selling lubricants. Gwyneth Paltrow is talking about hormone replacement therapy. Drug companies are paying for ghostwritten articles about it. But when does it become fearmongering?

entry.title: Hantavirus outbreak kills 3 on cruise ship in the Atlantic Ocean, WHO says
entry.summary: Hantaviruses are usually spread by exposure to urine, saliva or feces from infected rodents, such as rats or mice. Hantavirus infections are rare but can cause deadly respiratory infections.

entry.title: A New Atlas Reveals Hidden Details of The Human Body Like Never Before
entry.summary: A pioneering project has revealed the human body like never before, from entire organs down to cellular structures, with unprecedented precision on the scale of a single micron – about 50 times thinner than a strand of human hair.

entry.title: 1 in 4 people are ‘Alzheimer’s gene’ carriers like Chris Hemsworth. What to do
entry.summary: APOE4 comes with a higher risk for Alzheimer’s disease, but the 25 per cent of people who have it can lower that risk with lifestyle changes.

entry.title: A Simple Molecule Could Unlock Safer, Easier Weight Loss
entry.summary: Scientists have identified a compound that promotes weight loss without the bone damage seen in restrictive diets.

entry.title: Millions are losing weight on Ozempic and Wegovy — here's what an orthopedic surgeon wants every patient to know
entry.summary: Losing weight on Ozempic? An orthopedic surgeon explains what GLP-1 drugs really do to your bones, joints, and muscles and how to stay safe.

entry.title: Syphilis spikes in California as health chiefs warn sexually active to urgently get tested
entry.summary: Across the country, syphilis cases have risen sharply over the past decade.

entry.title: Routine hernia repair turns into deadly constipation due to medical malpractice: Lawsuit
entry.summary: An Iowa surgeon and two registered nurses are being sued by the family of a woman who say she died after a routine hernia repair turned into a deadly bout of constipation due to medical malpractice.

entry.title: Three dead in suspected virus outbreak on Atlantic cruise ship
entry.summary: The World Health Organization said one case of hantavirus infection has been confirmed, with five more suspected cases under investigation.

entry.title: Immune cells leave ‘memory’ of obesity after weight loss
entry.summary: The author of the study said the body's "memory" could still leave people who have lost weight at risk of health issues associated with obesity.

entry.title: Spread of disease from animals to humans is being driven by wildlife trade, investigation shows
entry.summary: "As long as we continue trading species, we will expose ourselves to this problem."

entry.title: Researchers Unveil Groundbreaking Sustainable Solution to Vitamin B12 Deficiency
entry.summary: A modified form of Spirulina may offer a more sustainable source of vitamin B12.

entry.title: How to spot and prevent the deadliest form of skin cancer
entry.summary: If caught early, it has a 99% cure rate.

entry.title: Family data reveal two genetic paths to childhood depression and anxiety
entry.summary: Many common mental health disorders, including depression and anxiety, are associated with a tendency to internalize problems or, in other words, to direct feelings inwards instead of expressing them and sharing them with others. Past studies suggest that thi…

entry.title: Scientists just discovered what coffee is really doing to your gut and brain
entry.summary: Coffee doesn’t just energize—it actively reshapes the gut and mind. Researchers found that both caffeinated and decaf coffee altered gut bacteria in ways linked to better mood and lower stress. Decaf even improved learning and memory, while caffeine boosted f…

entry.title: My Boyfriend Turned My Biggest Fantasy Into My Worst Nightmare. I’m This Close to Ending It.
entry.summary: It was supposed to be the group sex of our dreams.

entry.title: Heart doctor shares warning over food ‘worse than smoking’ that decreases lifespan
entry.summary: Eating high amounts is linked to a greater risk of high blood pressure, cardiovascular disease, heart attacks and strokes, and early death

entry.title: 4 Daily Exercises That Firm Belly Overhang Faster Than Gym Workouts After 60
entry.summary: An athletic trainer shares 4 daily moves that strengthen your deep core and reduce belly overhang, especially for adults over 60.
